foreground.h(3) Library Functions Manual foreground.h(3)

NAME

foreground.h

SYNOPSIS

Functions


char * image_print (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_256color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_color_utf8 (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color_dithered (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color_dithered_with_background (const image_t *image, bool use_background, const char *palette)

Function Documentation

char * image_print (const image_t * p, const char * palette)

Definition at line 27 of file foreground.c.

27                                                          {
28   if (!p || !palette) {
29     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: p=%p or palette=%p is NULL", p, palette);
30     return NULL;
31   }
32   if (!p->pixels) {
33     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: p->pixels is NULL");
34     return NULL;
35   }
36 
37   const int h = p->h;
38   const int w = p->w;
39 
40   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
41     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
42     return NULL;
43   }
44 
45   // Get UTF-8 character cache for proper multi-byte character support
46   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
47   if (!utf8_cache) {
48     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 palette cache for scalar rendering");
49     return NULL;
50   }
51 
52   // Character index ramp is now part of UTF-8 cache - no separate cache needed
53 
54   // Need space for h rows with UTF-8 characters, plus h-1 newlines, plus null terminator
55   const size_t max_char_bytes = 4; // Max UTF-8 character size
56 
57   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
58 
59   // Use outbuf_t for efficient UTF-8 RLE emission (same as SIMD renderers)
60   outbuf_t ob = {0};
61 
62   // Calculate buffer size with overflow checking
63   size_t w_times_bytes;
64   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)w, max_char_bytes, &w_times_bytes) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
65     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: width too large for UTF-8 encoding");
66     return NULL;
67   }
68 
69   size_t w_times_bytes_plus_one;
70   if (checked_size_add(w_times_bytes, 1, &w_times_bytes_plus_one) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
71     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: width * bytes + 1 overflow");
72     return NULL;
73   }
74 
75   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, w_times_bytes_plus_one, &ob.cap) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
76     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: height * (width * bytes + 1) overflow");
77     return NULL;
78   }
79 
80   ob.buf = SAFE_MALLOC(ob.cap ? ob.cap : 1, char *);
81   if (!ob.buf) {
82     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate output buffer for scalar rendering");
83     return NULL;
84   }
85 
86   // Process pixels with UTF-8 RLE emission (same approach as SIMD)
87   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
88     const int row_offset = y * w;
89 
90     for (int x = 0; x < w;) {
91       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
92       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
93       const int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
94 
95       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
96       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
97       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
98       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
99 
100       // Use same 64-entry cache as SIMD for consistency
101       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache64[luma_idx];
102 
103       // Find run length for same character (RLE optimization)
104       int j = x + 1;
105       while (j < w) {
106         const rgb_pixel_t next_pixel = pix[row_offset + j];
107         const int next_luminance = (77 * next_pixel.r + 150 * next_pixel.g + 29 * next_pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
108         uint8_t next_safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(next_luminance);
109         uint8_t next_luma_idx = (uint8_t)(next_safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
110         uint8_t next_char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[next_luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
111         if (next_char_idx != char_idx)
112           break;
113         j++;
114       }
115       uint32_t run = (uint32_t)(j - x);
116 
117       // Emit UTF-8 character with RLE (same as SIMD)
118       ob_write(&ob, (const char *)char_info->utf8_bytes, char_info->byte_len);
119       if (rep_is_profitable(run)) {
120         emit_rep(&ob, run - 1);
121       } else {
122         for (uint32_t k = 1; k < run; k++) {
123           ob_write(&ob, (const char *)char_info->utf8_bytes, char_info->byte_len);
124         }
125       }
126       x = j;
127     }
128 
129     // Add newline between rows (except last row)
130     if (y != h - 1) {
131       ob_putc(&ob, '\n');
132     }
133   }
134 
135   ob_term(&ob);
136   return ob.buf;
137 }








char
  * image_print_16color (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 526 of file foreground.c.


526                                                                      {
527   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
528     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
529     return NULL;
530   }
531 
532   int h = image->h;
533   int w = image->w;
534 
535   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
536     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
537     return NULL;
538   }
539 
540   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
541   ansi_fast_init_16color();
542 
543   // Calculate buffer size (smaller than 256-color due to shorter ANSI sequences)
544   // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
545   // Calculate with overflow checking
546   size_t h_times_w;
547   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
548     return NULL;
549   }
550 
551   size_t h_times_w_times_12;
552   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w, 12u, &h_times_w_times_12) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
553     return NULL;
554   }
555 
556   size_t buffer_size;
557   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w_times_12, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
558     return NULL;
559   }
560 
561   char *buffer;
562   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
563 
564   char *ptr = buffer;
565   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
566 
567   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
568   if (!utf8_cache) {
569     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color rendering");
570     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
571     return NULL;
572   }
573 
574   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
575     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
576       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
577 
578       // Convert RGB to 16-color index and generate ANSI sequence
579       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b);
580       ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
581 
582       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
583       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
584 
585       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
586       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
587       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
588       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
589 
590       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
591       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
592       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
593 
594       if (char_info) {
595         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
596         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
597           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
598         }
599       } else {
600         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
601         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
602         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
603         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
604       }
605     }
606 
607     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
608     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
609     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
610     if (y < h - 1) {
611       *ptr++ = '\n';
612     }
613   }
614 
615   *ptr = '\0';
616   return buffer;
617 }










char
  * image_print_16color_dithered (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 643 of file foreground.c.


643                                                                               {
644   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
645     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
646     return NULL;
647   }
648 
649   int h = image->h;
650   int w = image->w;
651 
652   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
653     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color_dithered: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
654     return NULL;
655   }
656 
657   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
658   ansi_fast_init_16color();
659 
660   // Allocate error buffer for Floyd-Steinberg dithering
661   size_t pixel_count = (size_t)h * (size_t)w;
662   rgb_error_t *error_buffer;
663   error_buffer = SAFE_CALLOC(pixel_count, sizeof(rgb_error_t), rgb_error_t *);
664 
665   // Calculate buffer size (same as non-dithered version)
666   // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
667   // Calculate with overflow checking
668   size_t h_times_w2;
669   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w2) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
670     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
671     return NULL;
672   }
673 
674   size_t h_times_w2_times_12;
675   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w2, 12u, &h_times_w2_times_12) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
676     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
677     return NULL;
678   }
679 
680   size_t buffer_size;
681   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w2_times_12, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
682     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
683     return NULL;
684   }
685 
686   char *buffer;
687   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
688 
689   char *ptr = buffer;
690   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
691 
692   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
693   if (!utf8_cache) {
694     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color dithered rendering");
695     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
696     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
697     return NULL;
698   }
699 
700   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
701     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
702       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
703 
704       // Convert RGB to 16-color index using dithering
705       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color_dithered(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b, x, y, w, h, error_buffer);
706       ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
707 
708       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
709       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
710 
711       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
712       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
713       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
714       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
715 
716       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
717       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
718       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
719 
720       if (char_info) {
721         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
722         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
723           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
724         }
725       } else {
726         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
727         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
728         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
729         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
730       }
731     }
732 
733     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
734     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
735     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
736     if (y < h - 1) {
737       *ptr++ = '\n';
738     }
739   }
740 
741   *ptr = '\0';
742   SAFE_FREE(error_buffer); // Clean up error buffer
743   return buffer;
744 }










char
  * image_print_16color_dithered_with_background (const image_t * image,
  bool use_background, const char * palette)


Definition at line 747 of file foreground.c.


747                                                                                                                    {
748   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
749     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
750     return NULL;
751   }
752 
753   int h = image->h;
754   int w = image->w;
755 
756   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
757     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color_dithered_with_background: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
758     return NULL;
759   }
760 
761   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
762   ansi_fast_init_16color();
763 
764   // Allocate error buffer for Floyd-Steinberg dithering
765   size_t pixel_count = (size_t)h * (size_t)w;
766   rgb_error_t *error_buffer;
767   error_buffer = SAFE_CALLOC(pixel_count, sizeof(rgb_error_t), rgb_error_t *);
768 
769   // Calculate buffer size (larger for background mode due to more ANSI sequences)
770   size_t buffer_size =
771       (size_t)h * (size_t)w * (use_background ? 24 : 12) + (size_t)h; // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
772   char *buffer;
773   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
774 
775   char *ptr = buffer;
776   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
777 
778   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
779   if (!utf8_cache) {
780     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color dithered bg rendering");
781     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
782     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
783     return NULL;
784   }
785 
786   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
787     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
788       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
789 
790       // Convert RGB to 16-color index using dithering
791       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color_dithered(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b, x, y, w, h, error_buffer);
792 
793       if (use_background) {
794         // Background mode: use contrasting foreground on background color
795         uint8_t bg_r, bg_g, bg_b;
796         get_16color_rgb(color_index, &bg_r, &bg_g, &bg_b);
797 
798         // Calculate luminance to choose contrasting foreground
799         int bg_luminance = (bg_r * 77 + bg_g * 150 + bg_b * 29) / 256;
800         uint8_t fg_color = (bg_luminance < 127) ? 15 : 0; // White on dark, black on bright
801 
802         ptr = append_16color_bg(ptr, color_index); // Set background to pixel color
803         ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, fg_color);    // Set contrasting foreground
804       } else {
805         // Foreground mode: set foreground to pixel color
806         ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
807       }
808 
809       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
810       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
811 
812       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
813       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
814       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);
815       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx];
816 
817       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
818       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
819       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
820 
821       if (char_info) {
822         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
823         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
824           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
825         }
826       } else {
827         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
828         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
829         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
830         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
831       }
832     }
833 
834     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
835     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
836     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
837     if (y < h - 1) {
838       *ptr++ = '\n';
839     }
840   }
841 
842   *ptr = '\0';
843   SAFE_FREE(error_buffer); // Clean up error buffer
844   return buffer;
845 }










char
  * image_print_256color (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)

421                                                                       {
422   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
423     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
424     return NULL;
425   }
426 
427   int h = image->h;
428   int w = image->w;
429 
430   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
431     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_256color: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
432     return NULL;
433   }
434 
435   ansi_fast_init_256color();
436 
437   // Buffer size: 256-color ANSI codes are "\033[38;5;NNNm" (max 13 bytes) + char + reset + newline
438   size_t h_times_w;
439   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
440     return NULL;
441   }
442 
443   size_t h_times_w_times_18;
444   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w, 18u, &h_times_w_times_18) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
445     return NULL;
446   }
447 
448   size_t buffer_size;
449   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w_times_18, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
450     return NULL;
451   }
452 
453   char *buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
454   char *ptr = buffer;
455   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
456 
457   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
458   if (!utf8_cache) {
459     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 256-color rendering");
460     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
461     return NULL;
462   }
463 
464   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
465     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
466       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
467 
468       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_256color(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b);
469       ptr = append_256color_fg(ptr, color_index);
470 
471       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
472 
473       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
474       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);
475       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx];
476 
477       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
478       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
479 
480       if (char_info) {
481         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
482           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
483         }
484       } else {
485         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
486         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
487         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
488       }
489     }
490 
491     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
492     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
493     if (y < h - 1) {
494       *ptr++ = '\n';
495     }
496   }
497 
498   *ptr = '\0';
499   return buffer;
500 }










char
  * image_print_color (const image_t * p, const char * palette)


Converts an image to colored ASCII art with ANSI escape codes.




This function generates a string representation of an image where
    each pixel is converted to an ASCII character with ANSI color codes. The
    character is chosen based on luminance, and colors are applied using 24-bit
    RGB ANSI escape sequences.




Buffer allocation is precisely calculated to avoid waste and
    prevent overflows:


    
  
  • Each pixel: 1 ASCII char + foreground ANSI code (19 bytes max)
    • 
  
  • Background mode: adds background ANSI code (19 bytes max per pixel)
    • 
  
  • Each row: reset sequence (\033[0m = 4 bytes) + newline (except last
    row)
    • 
  
  • At the end: null terminator (1 byte)
    • 



Color modes:


    
  
  • Foreground only (default): ASCII characters with colored foreground
    • 
  
  • Background mode (opt_background_color): colored background with
      contrasting foreground (black on bright backgrounds, white on dark
      backgrounds)
    • 



ANSI escape code format:


    
  
  • Foreground: \033[38;2;R;G;Bm (11-19 bytes depending on RGB values)
    • 
  
  • Background: \033[48;2;R;G;Bm (11-19 bytes depending on RGB values)
    • 
  
  • Reset: \033[0m (4 bytes)
    • 



Parameters


p Pointer to image_t structure containing
  pixel data


Returns


Dynamically allocated string containing colored ASCII
  art, or NULL on error. Caller is responsible for freeing the returned
  string.


Note


The function performs overflow checks to prevent integer
  overflow when calculating buffer sizes for very large images.



Uses global opt_background_color to determine color mode.




Exits with ERROR_BUFFER if buffer overflow is detected during
    string construction (should never happen with correct calculation).




Definition at line 194 of file foreground.c.


194                                                                {
195   if (!p || !palette) {
196     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "p=%p or palette=%p is NULL", p, palette);
197     return NULL;
198   }
199   if (!p->pixels) {
200     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "p->pixels is NULL");
201     return NULL;
202   }
203 
204   // Get UTF-8 character cache for proper multi-byte character support
205   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
206   if (!utf8_cache) {
207     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 palette cache for scalar color rendering");
208     return NULL;
209   }
210 
211   const int h = p->h;
212   const int w = p->w;
213 
214   // Constants for ANSI escape codes (using exact sizes from ansi_fast.c)
215   const size_t max_fg_ansi = 19; // \033[38;2;255;255;255m
216   const size_t max_bg_ansi = 19; // \033[48;2;255;255;255m
217   const size_t reset_len = 4;    // \033[0m
218 
219   const size_t h_sz = (size_t)h;
220   const size_t w_sz = (size_t)w;
221 
222   // Ensure h * w won't overflow
223   if (h_sz > 0 && w_sz > SIZE_MAX / h_sz) {
224     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Image dimensions too large: %d x %d", h, w);
225     return NULL;
226   }
227 
228   const size_t total_pixels = h_sz * w_sz;
229   const size_t bytes_per_pixel = 1 + max_fg_ansi + max_bg_ansi; // Conservative estimate for max possible
230 
231   // Ensure total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel won't overflow
232   if (total_pixels > SIZE_MAX / bytes_per_pixel) {
233     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Pixel data too large for buffer: %d x %d", h, w);
234     return NULL;
235   }
236 
237   const size_t pixel_bytes = total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel;
238 
239   // Per row: newline (except last row)
240   // Final reset added once by ansi_rle_finish()
241   const size_t total_newlines = (h_sz > 0) ? (h_sz - 1) : 0;
242   const size_t final_reset = reset_len; // One reset at end (from ansi_rle_finish)
243 
244   // Final buffer size: pixel bytes + per-row newlines + final reset + null terminator
245   const size_t extra_bytes = total_newlines + final_reset + 1;
246 
247   if (pixel_bytes > SIZE_MAX - extra_bytes) {
248     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Final buffer size would overflow: %d x %d", h, w);
249     return NULL;
250   }
251 
252   const size_t lines_size = pixel_bytes + extra_bytes;
253   char *lines;
254   lines = SAFE_MALLOC(lines_size, char *);
255 
256   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
257   // char *current_pos = lines;
258   // const char *buffer_end = lines + lines_size - 1; // reserve space for '\0'
259 
260   // Initialize the optimized RLE context for color sequence caching
261   // Note: This function should be called via image_print_with_capabilities() for proper per-client rendering
262   ansi_rle_context_t rle_ctx;
263   ansi_color_mode_t color_mode = ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND; // Default to foreground-only for legacy usage
264   ansi_rle_init(&rle_ctx, lines, lines_size, color_mode);
265 
266   // Process each pixel using the optimized RLE context
267   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
268     const int row_offset = y * w;
269 
270     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
271       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
272       int r = pixel.r, g = pixel.g, b = pixel.b;
273       // Standard ITU-R BT.601 luminance calculation (uses original values for accuracy)
274       const int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
275 
276       // Use UTF-8 character cache for proper character selection
277       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
278       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[safe_luminance];
279 
280       // For RLE, we need to pass the first byte of the UTF-8 character
281       // Note: RLE system may need updates for full UTF-8 support
282       const char ascii_char = char_info->utf8_bytes[0];
283 
284       ansi_rle_add_pixel(&rle_ctx, (uint8_t)r, (uint8_t)g, (uint8_t)b, ascii_char);
285     }
286 
287     // Add newline after each row (except the last row)
288     if (y != h - 1 && rle_ctx.length < rle_ctx.capacity - 1) {
289       rle_ctx.buffer[rle_ctx.length++] = '\n';
290     }
291   }
292 
293   ansi_rle_finish(&rle_ctx);
294 
295   return lines;
296 }










char
  * image_print_color_utf8 (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 300 of file foreground.c.


300                                                                     {
301   if (!p || !palette || !p->pixels) {
302     return NULL;
303   }
304 
305   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
306   if (!utf8_cache) {
307     return NULL;
308   }
309 
310   const int h = p->h;
311   const int w = p->w;
312   const size_t h_sz = (size_t)h;
313   const size_t w_sz = (size_t)w;
314 
315   if (h_sz > 0 && w_sz > SIZE_MAX / h_sz) {
316     return NULL;
317   }
318 
319   const size_t bytes_per_pixel = 30; // fg sequence (~19) + char (up to 4) + margin
320   const size_t total_pixels = h_sz * w_sz;
321   const size_t lines_size = total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel + h_sz + 16;
322 
323   char *lines;
324   lines = SAFE_MALLOC(lines_size, char *);
325 
326   char *ptr = lines;
327   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
328 
329   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
330     const int row_offset = y * w;
331 
332     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
333       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
334 
335       ptr += SAFE_SNPRINTF(ptr, 20, "\033[38;2;%d;%d;%dm", pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b);
336 
337       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
338       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
339       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[safe_luminance];
340       for (int i = 0; i < char_info->byte_len; i++) {
341         *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[i];
342       }
343     }
344 
345     *ptr++ = '\033';
346     *ptr++ = '[';
347     *ptr++ = '0';
348     *ptr++ = 'm';
349     if (y < h - 1) {
350       *ptr++ = '\n';
351     }
352   }
353 
354   *ptr = '\0';
355   return lines;
356 }






References rgb_pixel_t::b, utf8_char_t::byte_len,
    utf8_palette_cache::cache, rgb_pixel_t::g,
    get_utf8_palette_cache(), image_t::h, image_t::pixels,
    rgb_pixel_t::r, SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_SNPRINTF,
    utf8_char_t::utf8_bytes, and image_t::w.


Referenced by ascii_convert(), and
    image_print_with_capabilities().
